name: Borscht from Kiev
recipeIngredient: 16 oz plum tomatoes, skinned & coarsely chopped
1/2 cup sour cream or plain yogurt
recipeInstructions:
1. Place all ingredients marked with the  symbol in a small cloth bag. Tie the bag shut and place into the stock pot.
2. Add meat bones, meat, and water to a large stockpot and bring to a boil over high heat.
3. Skim the foam as needed.
4. Add the remaining stock ingredients, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 1 hour.
5. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
6. Wash, dry, and peel the beets. Wrap them in aluminum foil and bake in the oven for 1 hour 15 minutes.
7. Remove from the oven, allow to cool, and dice approximately 1/4".
8. Remove the ham bone, meat, and marrow bones from the stock. Set the marrow bones aside.
9. Strain the stock through a fine sieve into a clean pot. Discard the solids.
10. Bring the strained stock to a boil, then add tomatoes, potatoes, salt, and pepper; cook for 10 minutes on low heat covered.
11. Cook onions, carrots, and bell peppers in a cast-iron skillet for approximately 5 minutes.
12. Stir in cabbage and continue cooking vegetables for another 10 minutes.
13. Remove the cooked vegetables from the heat and add to the stock.
14. Sprinkle lemon juice over beets and add them to the stock.
15. Add tomatoes, tomato paste, and honey to the stock; cook until meat is fully incorporated (approximately 15 more minutes).
16. Remove from heat and serve with a generous dollop of sour cream on top.
